content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reconfigured to act as a persistent "curiosity backbone" that resolves a longstanding flaw of intrinsic reward models like ICM and RND: the tendency for novelty to decay as the agent becomes familiar with an environment. By hierarchically storing unlabeled, surprising transitions in structured episodic memory and actively querying only the highest-value uncompressed surprise memories for reprocessing, the agent maintains a sustained drive to explore instead of plateuing on intrinsic motivation.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
